summary: Pseudo-Dionysius: Mystical Theology + Divine Names — urtext of apophatic_theology
description:
**Parent:** Corpus Expansion Tier 1 (child 5 of 8)

## Source

- **Translator / license:** C.E. Rolt, SPCK 1920 (translator d. 1917). Clean US-PD.
- **URL / structure:** index https://www.ccel.org/ccel/rolt/dionysius.toc.html
  - **Mystical Theology** = single page, 5 short internal chapters (the "Divine Gloom" / affirmative-vs-negative core).
  - **Divine Names** = 13 chapters served as separate pages (`dionysius.iv.ii.html` … `dionysius.iv.xiv.html`).

## Likely 2 source entries

- `dionysius-mystical-theology` — `format = "html"`, `regex-section-split` on `CHAPTER I…V`.
- `dionysius-divine-names` — `format = "html_multi"`, `page-as-chunk` (one chapter per page).

## Concept payload

The **urtext of `apophatic_theology`** and a major `emanation_hierarchy` source. Sits exactly between Neoplatonism already held (Plotinus) and Christian mysticism already held (Eckhart, Boehme).

## Optional follow-on

The Celestial / Ecclesiastical Hierarchies are not in Rolt; take those from the **John Parker 1897** CCEL collection (`https://ccel.org/ccel/dionysius/celestial/celestial.ix.html`, pattern `celestial.<roman>.html`, PD) if wanted. Defer as a separate ticket.

## Ref

`docs/corpus-expansion-candidates.md` §3.5
